learning_outcomes.description: Appreciate & understand the scope of scientific inquiry in Ecology
Describe ecological processes as they apply to individuals, populations, species interactions, habitats and landscapes
Demonstrate understanding of the variety of ways that organisms interact with both the physical and the biological environment
Use techniques of data acquisition and analysis relevant to ecological questions
Appreciate the natural world through direct experience with local ecosystems as well using techniques for gathering data in the field
Become more proficient in writing a scientific paper in Journal format for publication

assessments.due_date:
1)Tutorial Workshop 1: 15/05/2026 4:00 pm

assessments.task_description:
Workshops are a core component of the learning and assessment for this course. Workshop assessments will be completed in person during the workshop in the allocated workshop time.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessed in person at the end of the tutorial
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The exam will be based on lecture material and first workshop. 

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
The field notebook will record accurate notes of all research done during the field trip and provide answers to questions posed in the Field notebook. The book should be completed on the field trip and the last page will be signed by tutors before you leave the Island. You can retain your notebook till the due date of the report in case you need to refer to the notes for the report write up.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submitted to tutors on the last day of the field trip.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Write up the data collected by the class from one of the activity modules (field project undertaken on K'Gari) as a scientific paper for publication. An outline of the report needs to be completed on the Island if you want to receive feedback before the due date. We will discuss in class how to write a paper for publication. Groups of 2-4 will be mandatory.

Reports are submitted 2 weeks after the completion of the field trip.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assessment must be submitted through Turnitin on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 9(16 Mar - 03 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Lectures

L1 Introduction & course logistics; Lecturer: Matthew Luskin

L2 Ecological Cascades (Lecture 2); Lecturer: Matthew Luskin

L3 Background to K'gari soils; Lecturer: Susanne Schmidt

L4 Background to K'gari plant ecology; Lecturer: Susanne Schmidt

L5 K'gari invertebrates; Lecturer: Mike Furlong

L6 Sampling invertebrates; Lecturer: Mike Furlong

L7 Sampling wildlife; Lecturer: Matthew Luskin

L8 Estimating wildlife distribution & abundance; Lecturer: Matthew Luskin

L9- Species interactions, predator ecology; Lecturer: Matthew Luskin

L10- Kgari Indigenous history and modern perspectives; Lecturer Steve Salisbury & guest

*Lectures are 1h each, and often there will be 2 lectures per class
